name: Foix Reservoir and Castellet Castle Loop
name_original: Embalse de Foix - Circular hasta el Castellet
description: Hike the moderate 9.9-mile Embalse de Foix circular trail to Castellet Castle, enjoying reservoir views and historical sites.
region_intro: Hiking around Castellet i la Gornal offers routes through a landscape characterized by the Foix Reservoir, historic Castellet Castle, and the surrounding Parc del Foix. The region features a mix of forests, vineyards, and open plains, providing varied terrain for outdoor activities. Elevated points offer views of the Penedès plain and the Mediterranean Sea. Best hiking trails around Castellet I La Gornal The most popular hiking route is Foix Reservoir and Castellet Castle Loop, a 9.9 miles (15.9 km) trail that takes 4 hours 37 minutes to complete. This moderate route combines scenic reservoir views with the historic Castellet Castle. Another top favourite among local hikers is Foix Reservoir Loop Trail, a moderate 7.0 miles (11.3 km) path. This trail explores the tranquil environment of the Foix Reservoir. Local hikers also love the La Font d'Hort Trailhead – Font d'Horta loop from Masia la Creu, a 2.6 miles (4.3 km) trail leading through the verdant forest surrounding the Foix reservoir, often completed in about 1 hour 16 minutes. Hiking around Castellet i la Gornal is defined by the Foix Reservoir, historic Castellet Castle, and the Penedès plain, with a network of routes offering options for various ability levels. The routes in Castellet i la Gornal are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 350 reviews. More than 1700 hikers have used komoot to explore Castellet i la Gornal's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Castellet i la Gornal?
A: There are over 30 hiking trails in Castellet i la Gornal, offering a variety of options for different skill levels. These include 7 easy routes, 21 moderate routes, and 2 more challenging trails.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Castellet i la Gornal?
A: Yes, Castellet i la Gornal offers several easy hiking trails perfect for beginners and families. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19831623" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Font d'Hort Trailhead – Font d'Horta loop from Masia la Creu&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 4.3 km long and takes around 1 hour 16 minutes through a verdant forest. The 'Ruta dels Cellers' (Winery Route) is also an easy, circular path through vineyards.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes around Castellet i la Gornal?
A: Yes, many popular routes in the area are circular. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e951881701/foix-reservoir-and-castellet-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foix Reservoir and Castellet Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a well-known circular trail, as is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e951213481/foix-reservoir-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foix Reservoir Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;. These allow you to start and end at the same point while enjoying the scenic beauty of the reservoir and the historic castle.

Q: What historical landmarks can I explore while hiking in Castellet i la Gornal?
A: The most prominent historical landmark is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/440250" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellet Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which dates back to 977 AD and overlooks the Foix Reservoir. Many trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e951881701/foix-reservoir-and-castellet-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foix Reservoir and Castellet Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt;, incorporate views of this impressive structure. The 'Ruta dels Castells de Marca' also connects several historic castles, including Castellet, offering a deeper dive into the region's defensive past.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints along the hiking trails in Castellet i la Gornal?
A: Yes, the region offers several stunning viewpoints. From elevated points like Coll de les Ventoses, hikers can enjoy panoramic views of the Penedès plain and even catch glimpses of the Mediterranean Sea. The trails around the Foix Reservoir also provide picturesque views of the water and the surrounding natural park, especially with Castellet Castle in the background.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Castellet i la Gornal?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 350 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscape, which combines tranquil reservoir views with historic landmarks like Castellet Castle, and the well-maintained paths through forests and vineyards. The variety of routes catering to different fitness levels is also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Castellet i la Gornal dog-friendly?
A: Many trails in Castellet i la Gornal are suitable for walking with dogs, particularly those through the natural park and around the reservoir. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in protected areas or near wildlife habitats, and to carry water for them.

Q: What natural features define the hiking experience in Castellet i la Gornal?
A: The hiking experience is largely defined by the tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4019675" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foix Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, which is a central natural feature and a habitat for waterfowl. The surrounding Parc del Foix offers diverse settings with forests and vineyards. From higher elevations, you can also enjoy views of the expansive Penedès plain.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through vineyards or offer wine-related experiences?
A: Yes, the 'Ruta dels Cellers' (Winery Route) is a circular trail designed for walking or cycling through rural and vineyard paths. This route allows you to visit local wineries such as Bodegues Pinord, Emendis, and Oriol Rossell, combining hiking with a taste of the renowned Penedès wine region.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Castellet i la Gornal?
A: The region is enjoyable for hiking throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, with blooming flowers or changing leaves. Even in winter, the mild Mediterranean climate can make for comfortable hiking days, though some trails might be muddy after rain.

Q: Are there any mountain peaks or summits accessible from the hiking trails?
A: While not towering mountains, the area around Castellet i la Gornal features several accessible peaks and elevated points. You can explore highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2579854" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig de l'Àliga&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3923019" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penya del Papiol&lt;/a&gt;, which offer rewarding views of the surrounding landscape and the Penedès plain.

Q: Can I reach the hiking trails using public transport?
A: While specific public transport connections directly to all trailheads might be limited, the town of Castellet i la Gornal itself is accessible by bus services from nearby larger towns. From there, some trailheads, especially those around the Foix Reservoir and Castellet Castle, may be within walking distance or a short taxi ride. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for the most up-to-date information.
tips:
This old farmhouse on the banks of the Foix reservoir is made up of numerous outbuildings and corrals. It is surrounded by a bastion or wall, which served to protect property from bandits.

A few meters from the farmhouse is the Cal Bladet viewpoint, from where you can observe the diversity of fauna and flora. It is a place very frequented by lovers of ornithology, since in addition to the typical birds of the riverside forest, aquatic birds are also observed.

The pantà de Foix was inaugurated in 1928 and was built to supply irrigation water to the orchards. It collects the waters of the river Foix, which flows into Cubelles. You can also see waterfowl from an observatory in front of the Foix reservoir. Don't forget to take your binoculars.

Located on the Sierra Mala, this abandoned farmhouse is another example of this type of construction in the area, next to the Casa Alta or Cal Cremat, now uninhabited due to the lack of water and the consequent drop in agricultural productivity.

Castellet Castle dates from the 10th century, originally little more than a large fortified house with a watchtower, it was intended to protect the Hispanic March of al-Andalus.

Over time and the successive occupants it did not fall into disuse thanks to its strategic position between Garraf and Penedès. Today it is a tourist attraction, thanks to its state of conservation and its privileged location on the meander of the Foix River, today occupied by the Foix reservoir. There is a terrace bar, very popular with bikers and cyclists, just off the road.

faqs.question: How difficult is the Embalse de Foix - Circular until the Castellet trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as moderate. It is suitable for hikers with good physical condition and steady footing. While most paths are accessible, expect some tiring slopes and sections with small stairs.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this circular route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily features dirt paths that are mostly accessible. You'll traverse natural environments, including Mediterranean white pine and oak forests. Be aware that approximately half of the trail lacks shade.

faqs.question: Are there any notable sights or landmarks along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail offers incredible views of the Foix Reservoir and its dam. Key highlights include the Cal Bladet and the Foix Reservoir Viewpoint, the expansive Foix Reservoir itself, the historical Ruins of Cal Balaguer, and the picturesque Castellet Castle.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery. In autumn, you can observe the vibrant hues of poplars and oaks contrasting with evergreen pine forests. During warmer months, be prepared for sun exposure on unshaded sections.

faqs.question: Is the Embalse de Foix - Circular until the Castellet trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, as it passes through natural areas and potentially near wildlife, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash to ensure their safety and respect for other hikers and the environment.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to hike in Parc del Foix?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail within the Parc del Foix. The park aims to protect its ecological and cultural value, so please respect all local regulations, stay on marked paths, and practice Leave No Trace principles.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details are not provided, trails around the Embalse de Foix typically have designated parking areas near the reservoir or the village of Castellet. Look for signs indicating public parking when you arrive.

faqs.question: Are there any water sources or facilities along the route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no potable water sources directly along the trail. It is crucial to carry sufficient water for your entire hike. There are also no public restrooms or facilities directly on the trail itself.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers significant sections of the Garraf (48%) and the Penedès 360 Mountain Bike (ruta 5 dies) (39%) trails. You'll also find smaller portions overlapping with the Variant: Desembocadura del Foix - Castell de Queralt (33%) and the Penedès 360 Trekking (27%) routes.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see around the Foix Reservoir?
faqs.answer: The Foix Reservoir is a vital natural space and a crucial refuge for numerous bird species and amphibians. Birdwatching is a popular activity, with opportunities to spot various aquatic birds, sometimes from an observatory. The area is a unique wetland in a generally dry region.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and lack of shade on half the trail, you should bring plenty of water, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), appropriate hiking footwear, and snacks. A map or GPS device is also recommended as the path is typically marked with green and white signs.

waypoints.name: Cal Bladet and the Foix Reservoir Viewpoint
